name: ExtensionEncounter_SubjectStatus
title: R5: The current status of the subject in relation to the Encounter (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Encounter.subjectStatus` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Encounter.subjectStatus` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Encounter.subjectStatus` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Encounter.subjectStatus` has a context of Encounter based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Encounter`.
Element `Encounter.subjectStatus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The current status of the subject in relation to the Encounter (new)
element.comment:
Element `Encounter.subjectStatus` has a context of Encounter based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Encounter`.
Element `Encounter.subjectStatus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Different use-cases are likely to have different permitted transitions between states, such as an Emergency department could use `arrived` when the patient first presents, then `triaged` once has been assessed by a nurse, then `receiving-care` once treatment begins, however other sectors may use a different set of these values, or their own custom set in place of this example valueset provided.

element.short: The current status of the subject in relation to the Encounter
element.definition: The subjectStatus value can be used to track the patient's status within the encounter. It details whether the patient has arrived or departed, has been triaged or is currently in a waiting status.
element.comment: Different use-cases are likely to have different permitted transitions between states, such as an Emergency department could use `arrived` when the patient first presents, then `triaged` once has been assessed by a nurse, then `receiving-care` once treatment begins, however other sectors may use a different set of these values, or their own custom set in place of this example valueset provided.

binding.description: Current status of the subject  within the encounter.
